evidence.quote: Experience with observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, OpenTelemetry, etc.).

tools.notes: Sierra's own data lakehouse table format for its Agent Data Platform.

evidence.quote: This includes our real-time eventing infrastructure, streaming and batch ETL pipelines, and our Iceberg-based data lakehouse.

tools.notes: Sierra deliberately avoids single-vendor lock-in: agents are assembled from 15+ frontier, open-weight, and proprietary models, routed per task (latency-sensitive tool calling, classification, long-context reasoning, tone) by a custom orchestration layer. Sierra's own communications do not name the specific model vendors behind this system for the production platform.

evidence.quote: agents built on Sierra are assembled using 15+ frontier, open-weight, and proprietary models, depending on the job to be done... we call this a constellation of models

Sierra uses 15+ frontier and open source models for low latency tool calling and decision-making, precision classification, long-context reasoning, and empathy/tone. We call this a constellation of models

tools.notes: In-house always-on evaluation layer; each monitor goes through its own evaluation loop grounded in team-labeled conversations and multi-model agreement.

evidence.quote: Monitors, Sierra's always-on evaluation layer, use an LLM-as-judge to review every conversation so businesses can track agent quality and customer sentiment
